description_plain: Your child uses the sentence around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child uses different ways to figure out the meaning of new or tricky words when reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal and informal ways of speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries or other references to check and fix spelling when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (e.g., cage → badge; boy → boil).
description_plain: Your child uses spelling patterns they have learned when writing new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child uses apostrophes correctly to make contractions and show possession.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly in greetings and closings of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes holidays, brand names, and place names correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create readable documents with legible print.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly with neat and readable handwriting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences (e.g., The boy watched the movie; The little boy watched the movie; The action movie was watched by the little boy).
description_plain: Your child can write simple and compound sentences, and change or add to them to make them better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child uses describing words (adjectives) and words that tell how actions happen (adverbs) correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., sat, hid, told).
description_plain: Your child uses the past tense of common irregular verbs like sat, hid, and told.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses reflexive pronouns like myself and ourselves correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., feet, children, teeth, mice, fish).
description_plain: Your child uses irregular plural nouns like feet, children, and mice correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns (e.g., group).
description_plain: Your child uses collective nouns like group to talk about a collection of things or people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing, building on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and figures out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the text, breaking down word parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and chooses words and styles that fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child speaks in complete sentences when needed to give details or explain something clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can make audio recordings of stories or poems and add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and deliver a narrative presentation that: recounts a well-elaborated event, includes details, reflects a logical sequence, and provides a conclusion.
description_plain: Your child can plan and tell a story about an event with clear details, in order, and finish with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or share an experience using facts and details, speaking clearly in full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says to better understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Give and follow three- and four-step oral directions.
description_plain: Your child can listen to and follow oral directions that have three or four steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe important ideas or details from stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask for more explanation when they don’t understand what is being talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can join conversations by adding comments that connect to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group talks, like waiting their turn, listening carefully, and speaking one at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group conversations about grade 2 topics with classmates and adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child adjusts how they speak for different situations and uses formal English when appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and the style fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child judges a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different media like visuals, numbers, and speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child prepares for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and convincingly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and longer times for different tasks and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child can remember information from experiences or find facts from sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., read a number of books on a single topic to produce a report; record science observations).
description_plain: Your child can work with others on research and writing projects, like reading books on one topic to make a report.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child can use digital tools to create and share writing, and practice printing, cursive, or typing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: With support, your child can focus on a topic and improve their writing by revising and editing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose. (Grade-specific expectations for writing types are defined in standards 1–3 above.)
description_plain: With help, your child can write pieces that are organized and fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount a well-elaborated event or short sequence of events, include details to describe actions, thoughts, and feelings, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about events with details about actions and feelings, use words that show order, and end with a closing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they introduce a topic, use facts and definitions to develop points, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that introduce a topic, give facts and definitions, and end with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply reasons that support the opinion, use linking words (e.g., because, and, also) to connect opinion and reasons, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that state a topic and opinion, give reasons, use linking words, and finish with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over long and short times for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use details from stories or information texts to support their thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if they are trustworthy and accurate, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects by focusing on questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support claims about important topics using good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to check or fix how they read words, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level text out loud accurately, with good speed and expression, after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level text with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read grade-appropriate words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can identify words that have common but tricky spelling and sound patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words with common beginnings and endings (prefixes and suffixes).

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words with long vowel sounds that follow regular spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows the sounds for common vowel teams when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words both in isolation and in text.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word skills to read words alone and in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 2—3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade 2–3 informational texts about history, science, and technology, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare the main points from two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons support specific points the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how reasons in a text support the author’s main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images (e.g., a diagram showing how a machine works) contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures or diagrams help explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main purpose of a text, like what the author wants to explain or describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., captions, bold print, subheadings, glossaries, indexes, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use text features like captions, bold words, and indexes to find important information quickly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.(See grade 2 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases related to grade 2 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic of a multiparagraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main topic of a text and the focus of specific paragraphs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories and poetry, in the grades 2—3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories and poems for 2nd and 3rd grade levels, using help when needed, and connect what they read to their own experiences or other stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story (e.g., Cinderella stories) by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more versions of the same story by different authors or from different cultures and notice how they are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in books or on screens to understand the characters, where the story happens, and what happens in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge differences in the points of view of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud.
description_plain: Your child understands that characters have different points of view and can show this by using different voices when reading their dialogue out loud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of a story, including describing how the beginning introduces the story and the ending concludes the action.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story is put together, explaining how the beginning starts the story and the ending finishes it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases (e.g., regular beats, alliteration, rhymes, repeated lines) supply rhythm and meaning in a story, poem, or song.
description_plain: Your child can explain how words and phrases like rhymes, repeated lines, or beats add rhythm and meaning to stories, poems, or songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters react to important events and challenges in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine their central message, lesson, or moral.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories, including fables and folktales from different cultures, and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar ideas or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence in a text and decide if they make sense and are enough to support the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can use and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a text, like sentences or chapters, connect to each other and to the whole text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or technical meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make good guesses about what it means, using parts of the text to explain their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to hundredths by reasoning about their size. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two decimals refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using the number line or another visual model.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals to hundredths by thinking about their size, write >, =, or < to show the comparison, and explain why, using pictures or models.

subject: Math
description: Use decimal notation for fractions with denominators 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can write fractions with denominators of 10 or 100 as decimals.

subject: Math
description: Express a fraction with denominator 10 as an equivalent fraction with denominator 100, and use this technique to add two fractions with respective denominators 10 and 100.
description_plain: Your child can change a fraction with denominator 10 into an equal fraction with denominator 100, and use this to add fractions with denominators 10 and 100.

subject: Math
description: Understand decimal notation for fractions, and compare decimal fractions.
description_plain: Your child can understand how fractions and decimals are related and can compare decimal numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving multiplication of a fraction by a whole number, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that multiply a fraction by a whole number, using pictures or equations, and figure out between which two whole numbers the answer falls.

subject: Math
description: Understand a multiple of a/b as a multiple of 1/b, and use this understanding to multiply a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child understands that multiplying a fraction by a whole number means multiplying the numerator and can use this to find the product.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b as a multiple of 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction like a/b means a times the size of 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by a whole number using what they already know about multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole and having like denominators, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that add or subtract fractions with the same denominator.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract mixed numbers with like denominators, e.g., by replacing each mixed number with an equivalent fraction, and/or by using properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract mixed numbers with the same bottom number by changing them into improper fractions or using addition and subtraction rules.

subject: Math
description: Decompose a fraction into a sum of fractions with the same denominator in more than one way, recording each decomposition by an equation. Justify decompositions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
description_plain: Your child can break a fraction into a sum of smaller fractions with the same denominator in different ways and explain using pictures.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition and subtraction of fractions as joining and separating parts referring to the same whole.
description_plain: Your child understands adding and subtracting fractions as putting parts together or taking parts apart when they refer to the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b with a > 1 as a sum of fractions 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction with numerator greater than 1 is the same as adding several fractions with numerator 1.

subject: Math
description: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can build fractions by adding unit fractions and use what they know about whole numbers to do this.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators, e.g., by creating common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to a benchmark fraction such as 1/2. Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of comparisons with symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, e.g., by using a visual fraction model.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions with different top and bottom numbers by finding common denominators or comparing to ½, and explain which is bigger, smaller, or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction (n × a)/(n × b) by using visual fraction models, with attention to how the number and size of the parts differ even though the two fractions themselves are the same size. Use this principle to recognize and generate equivalent fractions.
description_plain: Your child can explain why two fractions are equal by using pictures that show how the parts are different sizes but the fractions are the same.

subject: Math
description: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and ordering.
description_plain: Your child understands how to tell if two fractions are equal and can put fractions in order from smallest to largest.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into matching parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.
description_plain: Your child can find and draw lines of symmetry on flat shapes, showing where the shape can be folded to match perfectly.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size. Recognize right triangles as a category, and identify right triangles. (Two-dimensional shapes should include special triangles, e.g., equilateral, isosceles, scalene, and special quadrilaterals, e.g., rhombus, square, rectangle, parallelogram, trapezoid.)
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes by whether they have parallel or perpendicular lines or certain angle sizes, and can recognize and name right triangles.

subject: Math
description: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child can draw points, lines, line segments, rays, different types of angles, and parallel or perpendicular lines, and find these in flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
description_plain: Your child can draw and identify lines and angles and group shapes based on their lines and angles.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angle measure as additive. When an angle is decomposed into non-overlapping parts, the angle measure of the whole is the sum of the angle measures of the parts. Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real world and mathematical problems, e.g., by using an equation with a symbol for the unknown angle measure.
description_plain: Your child understands that angles can be added together, and can solve problems to find missing angles by adding or subtracting angle measures.

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Sketch angles of specified measure.
description_plain: Your child can measure angles using a protractor and draw angles of a given size.

subject: Math
description: An angle that turns through n one-degree angles is said to have an angle measure of n degrees.
description_plain: Your child understands that an angle made by turning through n one-degree steps has a measure of n degrees.

subject: Math
description: An angle is measured with reference to a circle with its center at the common endpoint of the rays, by considering the fraction of the circular arc between the points where the two rays intersect the circle. An angle that turns through 1/360 of a circle is called a "one-degree angle," and can be used to measure angles.
description_plain: Your child knows that angles are measured by how much of a circle they cover, with one degree being 1/360 of a full turn.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angles as geometric shapes that are formed wherever two rays share a common endpoint, and understand concepts of angle measurement:
description_plain: Your child understands that angles are shapes made by two rays sharing a point, and learns how to measure them.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
description_plain: Your child learns about angles and how to measure them.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements using fractions and solve addition or subtraction problems using the data.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the area and perimeter of rectangles in real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations to solve word problems involving distances, intervals of time, liquid volumes, masses of objects, and money, including problems involving simple fractions or decimals, and problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Represent measurement quantities using diagrams such as number line diagrams that feature a measurement scale.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with measurements like distance, time, volume, weight, and money.

subject: Math
description: Know relative sizes of measurement units within one system of units including km, m, cm; kg, g; lb, oz.; l, ml; hr, min, sec. Within a single system of measurement, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Record measurement equivalents in a two column table.
description_plain: Your child knows how different units of measurement relate within the same system and can convert measurements from larger to smaller units and make a conversion table.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that involve measuring things and changing measurements from larger units to smaller units.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers up to four digits by one-digit numbers, find the quotient and remainder, and explain the steps using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number, and multiply two two-digit numbers, using strategies based on place value and the properties of operations. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a number up to four digits by a one-digit number and multiply two two-digit numbers, explaining their work with pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract large whole numbers using the standard method.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.
description_plain: Your child can use their understanding of place value to solve problems with large numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round large whole numbers to any place value using their understanding of place value.

subject: Math
description: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form. Compare two multi-digit numbers based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and compare large numbers using digits, words, and expanded form, and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in one place represents ten times what it represents in the place to its right.
description_plain: Your child understands that in a number, a digit in one place is ten times bigger than the same digit in the place to its right.

subject: Math
description: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child understands how place value works for numbers with more than one digit.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Find all factor pairs for a whole number in the range 1—100. Recognize that a whole number is a multiple of each of its factors. Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1—100 is a multiple of a given one-digit number. Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1—100 is prime or composite.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Solve multistep word problems posed with whole numbers and having whole-number answers using the four operations, including problems in which remainders must be interpreted. subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can read and understand literary nonfiction for grades 9-10, with help if needed, and by grade 10 can do this independently.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is valid and the evidence is relevant and sufficient; identify false statements and fallacious reasoning.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the cumulative impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone (e.g., how the language of a court opinion differs from that of a newspaper). (See grade 9–10 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone and meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description:
By the end of grade 9, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 9—10 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 10, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 9—10 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories, dramas, and poems for grades 9-10, using background knowledge to connect personally and culturally, and by grade 10 can do this independently.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or technical meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make good guesses about what it means, using parts of the text to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make good guesses about what it means, using parts of the text to explain their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Solve real world problems involving division of unit fractions by non-zero whole numbers and division of whole numbers by unit fractions, e.g., by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real problems dividing unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a whole number by a unit fraction, and compute such quotients.
description_plain: Your child understands and can calculate dividing a whole number by a unit fraction.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a unit fraction by a non-zero whole number, and compute such quotients.
description_plain: Your child understands and can calculate dividing a unit fraction by a whole number.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of division to divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child can divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions using strategies based on multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Solve real world problems involving multiplication of fractions and mixed numbers, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by multiplying fractions and mixed numbers using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction greater than 1 results in a product greater than the given number (recognizing multiplication by whole numbers greater than 1 as a familiar case); explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction less than 1 results in a product smaller than the given number; and relating the principle of fraction equivalence a/b = (n×a)/(n×b) to the effect of multiplying a/b by 1.
description_plain: Your child understands why multiplying by a fraction bigger than 1 makes a number bigger, and multiplying by a fraction smaller than 1 makes it smaller.

subject: Math
description: Comparing the size of a product to the size of one factor on the basis of the size of the other factor, without performing the indicated multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can compare the size of a multiplication answer to one of the numbers being multiplied, without actually multiplying.

subject: Math
description: Interpret multiplication as scaling (resizing), by:
description_plain: Your child understands multiplication as resizing or scaling numbers.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with fractional side lengths by tiling it with unit squares of the appropriate unit fraction side lengths, and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths. Multiply fractional side lengths to find areas of rectangles, and represent fraction products as rectangular areas.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of rectangles with fractional sides by counting small squares and by multiplying the side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the product (a/b) × q as a parts of a partition of q into b equal parts; equivalently, as the result of a sequence of operations a × q ÷ b.
description_plain: Your child understands multiplying a fraction by a number as taking parts of that number, using pictures to show this.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction or whole number by a fraction.
description_plain: Your child can multiply fractions and whole numbers by fractions, building on what they already know about multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b). Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction means dividing the top number by the bottom number and can solve word problems using this idea.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to multiply and divide fractions.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide fractions by building on what they already know about multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems adding and subtracting fractions with different denominators, using pictures or equations to help.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with different denominators by changing them to equivalent fractions with the same denominator.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions as a strategy to add and subtract fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use equivalent fractions to help add and subtract fractions.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes into groups based on their features, showing how some shapes are part of bigger groups.

subject: Math
description: Understand that attributes belonging to a category of two-dimensional figures also belong to all subcategories of that category.
description_plain: Your child understands that if a shape belongs to a group, it also has all the features of that group.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures into categories based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can group flat shapes into categories based on their features.

subject: Math
description: Represent real world and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane, and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on the first quadrant of a coordinate plane and explain what the points mean in real situations.

subject: Math
description: Use a pair of perpendicular number lines, called axes, to define a coordinate system, with the intersection of the lines (the origin) arranged to coincide with the 0 on each line and a given point in the plane located by using an ordered pair of numbers, called its coordinates. Understand that the first number indicates how far to travel from the origin in the direction of one axis, and the second number indicates how far to travel in the direction of the second axis, with the convention that the names of the two axes and the coordinates correspond (e.g., x-axis and x-coordinate, y-axis and y-coordinate).
description_plain: Your child understands how to use two number lines that cross to find and name points using pairs of numbers called coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Graph points on the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can put points on a graph to help solve real-world and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as additive. Find volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms by adding the volumes of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real world problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of solid shapes made by joining two rectangular boxes by adding their volumes, and use this to solve real problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas V = l × w × h and V = b × h for rectangular prisms to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of rectangular boxes with whole-number sides to solve real and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole-number side lengths by packing it with unit cubes, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths, equivalently by multiplying the height by the area of the base. Represent threefold whole-number products as volumes, e.g., to represent the associative property of multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of a rectangular box by filling it with unit cubes and show that multiplying its length, width, and height gives the same volume.

subject: Math
description: Relate volume to the operations of multiplication and addition and solve real world and mathematical problems involving volume.
description_plain: Your child understands that volume can be found by multiplying and adding, and can solve problems about volume.

subject: Math
description: Measure volumes by counting unit cubes, using cubic cm, cubic in, cubic ft, and improvised units.
description_plain: Your child can measure volume by counting unit cubes and use different units like cubic centimeters or cubic inches.

subject: Math
description: A solid figure which can be packed without gaps or overlaps using n unit cubes is said to have a volume of n cubic units.
description_plain: Your child knows that if a solid can be filled exactly with n unit cubes, its volume is n cubic units.

subject: Math
description: A cube with side length 1 unit, called a "unit cube," is said to have "one cubic unit" of volume, and can be used to measure volume.
description_plain: Your child understands that a cube with sides of 1 unit is called a unit cube and has a volume of one cubic unit.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as an attribute of solid figures and understand concepts of volume measurement.
description_plain: Your child understands that volume is how much space a solid figure takes up and learns how to measure it.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of volume and relate volume to multiplication and to addition.
description_plain: Your child learns about volume and how it connects to multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (1/2, 1/4, 1/8). Use operations on fractions for this grade to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements using fractions and use fraction math to solve problems from the plot.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Convert among different-sized standard measurement units within a given measurement system (e.g., convert 5 cm to 0.05 m), and use these conversions in solving multi-step, real world problems.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from one unit to another (like centimeters to meters) and use these changes to solve multi-step real problems.

subject: Math
description: Convert like measurement units within a given measurement system.
description_plain: Your child can convert between units that measure the same thing within one measurement system.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths using drawings and place value strategies, and explain how they solve these problems.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients of whole numbers with up to four-digit dividends and two-digit divisors, using strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division. Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can divide whole numbers up to four digits by two-digit numbers using place value and multiplication ideas, and explain their work with pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Fluently multiply multi-digit whole numbers using the standard algorithm.
description_plain: Your child can multiply large whole numbers quickly and correctly using the standard method.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with multi-digit whole numbers and with decimals to hundredths.
description_plain: Your child can work with large whole numbers and decimals to the hundredths place using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding to round decimals to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round decimal numbers to any place value using their understanding of place value.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to thousandths based on meanings of the digits in each place, using >, =, and < symbols to record the results of comparisons.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals up to thousandths place and use >, =, and < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Read and write decimals to thousandths using base-ten numerals, number names, and expanded form, e.g., 347.392 = 3 × 100 + 4 × 10 + 7 × 1 + 3 × (1/10) + 9 × (1/100) + 2 × (1/1000).
description_plain: Your child can read and write decimals to the thousandths place in numbers, words, and expanded form.

subject: Math
description: Read, write, and compare decimals to thousandths.
description_plain: Your child can read, write, and compare decimals up to the thousandths place.

subject: Math
description: Explain patterns in the number of zeros of the product when multiplying a number by powers of 10, and explain patterns in the placement of the decimal point when a decimal is multiplied or divided by a power of 10. Use whole-number exponents to denote powers of 10.
description_plain: Your child can explain patterns when multiplying or dividing numbers by 10, 100, 1000, and use exponents to show these powers of 10.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit number, a digit in one place represents 10 times as much as it represents in the place to its right and 1/10 of what it represents in the place to its left.
description_plain: Your child knows that in a number, each digit is ten times bigger than the digit to its right and one-tenth the size of the digit to its left.

subject: Math
description: Understand the place value system.
description_plain: Your child understands how place value works in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Generate two numerical patterns using two given rules. Identify apparent relationships between corresponding terms. Form ordered pairs consisting of corresponding terms from the two patterns, and graph the ordered pairs on a coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can create two number patterns from rules, find relationships between them, and graph the matching pairs on a coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Analyze patterns and relationships.
description_plain: Your child can find and understand patterns and relationships in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Express a whole number in the range 2–50 as a product of its prime factors. For example, find the prime factors of 24 and express 24 as 2 × 2 × 2 × 3.
description_plain: Your child can break down whole numbers from 2 to 50 into their prime factors, like writing 24 as 2 × 2 × 2 × 3.

subject: Math
description: Write simple expressions that record calculations with numbers, and interpret numerical expressions without evaluating them.
description_plain: Your child can write simple math expressions to show calculations and understand what the expressions mean without solving them.

subject: Math
description: Use parentheses, brackets, or braces in numerical expressions, and evaluate expressions with these symbols.
description_plain: Your child can use parentheses, brackets, or braces in math expressions and solve those expressions correctly.

subject: Math
description: Write and interpret numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child can write and understand math expressions.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Design and use a simulation to generate frequencies for compound events.
description_plain: Your child can create and use simulations to find how often combined events happen.

subject: Math
description: Represent sample spaces for compound events using methods such as organized lists, tables and tree diagrams. For an event described in everyday language (e.g., "rolling double sixes"), identify the outcomes in the sample space which compose the event.
description_plain: Your child can list all possible outcomes for combined events using organized methods and identify which outcomes match a described event.

subject: Math
description: Understand that, just as with simple events, the probability of a compound event is the fraction of outcomes in the sample space for which the compound event occurs.
description_plain: Your child understands that the chance of combined events happening is the fraction of all possible outcomes that fit the event.

subject: Math
description: Find probabilities of compound events using organized lists, tables, tree diagrams, and simulation.
description_plain: Your child can find the probability of combined events using lists, tables, tree diagrams, and simulations.

subject: Math
description: Develop a probability model (which may not be uniform) by observing frequencies in data generated from a chance process.
description_plain: Your child can create a probability model by watching how often things happen in chance activities, even if outcomes are not all equally likely.

subject: Math
description: Develop a uniform probability model by assigning equal probability to all outcomes, and use the model to determine probabilities of events.
description_plain: Your child can make a probability model where all outcomes are equally likely and use it to find the chance of events.

subject: Math
description: Develop a probability model and use it to find probabilities of events. Compare probabilities from a model to observed frequencies; if the agreement is not good, explain possible sources of the discrepancy.
description_plain: Your child can build a probability model to find chances of events and compare these to actual results, explaining why they might be different.

subject: Math
description: Approximate the probability of a chance event by collecting data on the chance process that produces it and observing its long-run relative frequency, and predict the approximate relative frequency given the probability.
description_plain: Your child can estimate the chance of an event by collecting data and observing how often it happens over time, then predict future chances.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the probability of a chance event is a number between 0 and 1 that expresses the likelihood of the event occurring. Larger numbers indicate greater likelihood. A probability near 0 indicates an unlikely event, a probability around 1/2 indicates an event that is neither unlikely nor likely, and a probability near 1 indicates a likely event.
description_plain: Your child understands that probability is a number from 0 to 1 showing how likely an event is, with numbers near 0 meaning unlikely and near 1 meaning very likely.

subject: Math
description: Investigate chance processes and develop, use, and evaluate probability models.
description_plain: Your child explores chance events and learns how to create, use, and check probability models.

subject: Math
description: Use measures of center and measures of variability for numerical data from random samples to draw informal comparative inferences about two populations.
description_plain: Your child can use averages and spread of data from random samples to make simple comparisons between two groups.

subject: Math
description: Informally assess the degree of visual overlap of two numerical data distributions with similar variabilities, measuring the difference between the centers by expressing it as a multiple of a measure of variability.
description_plain: Your child can look at two sets of data and estimate how much their values overlap and how different their centers are compared to their spread.

subject: Math
description: Draw informal comparative inferences about two populations.
description_plain: Your child makes informal comparisons between two groups using data.

subject: Math
description: Use data from a random sample to draw inferences about a population with an unknown characteristic of interest. Generate multiple samples (or simulated samples) of the same size to gauge the variation in estimates or predictions.
description_plain: Your child can use data from random samples to make guesses about a larger group and create several samples to see how much the guesses might change.

subject: Math
description: Understand that statistics can be used to gain information about a population by examining a sample of the population; generalizations about a population from a sample are valid only if the sample is representative of that population. Understand that random sampling tends to produce representative samples and support valid inferences.
description_plain: Your child understands that looking at a smaller group (sample) can tell us about a bigger group (population) only if the sample fairly represents the whole group.

subject: Math
description: Use random sampling to draw inferences about a population.
description_plain: Your child uses random samples to learn about larger groups.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems leading to inequalities of the form px + q > r or px + q < r, where p, q, and r are specific rational numbers. Graph the solution set of the inequality and interpret it in the context of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that lead to inequalities like px + q > r, graph the solutions, and explain what the graph means for the problem.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems leading to equations of the form px + q = r and p(x + q) = r, where p, q, and r are specific rational numbers. Solve equations of these forms fluently. Compare an algebraic solution to an arithmetic solution, identifying the sequence of the operations used in each approach.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that lead to equations like px + q = r or p(x + q) = r, solve them easily, and compare algebra and arithmetic methods.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent quantities in a real-world or mathematical problem, and construct simple equations and inequalities to solve problems by reasoning about the quantities.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for numbers in real or math problems and write simple equations or inequalities to solve them.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step real-life and mathematical problems posed with positive and negative rational numbers in any form (whole numbers, fractions, and decimals), using tools strategically. Apply properties of operations to calculate with numbers in any form; convert between forms as appropriate; and assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step problems with positive and negative numbers in different forms, using tools and checking if answers make sense.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-life and mathematical problems using numerical and algebraic expressions and equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems using number and algebra expressions and equations.

subject: Math
description: Understand that rewriting an expression in different forms in a problem context can shed light on the problem and how the quantities in it are related.
description_plain: Your child understands that changing how an expression looks can help understand the problem and how the numbers relate.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add, subtract, factor, and expand linear expressions with rational coefficients.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to add, subtract, factor, and expand expressions with rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child uses math properties to create expressions that mean the same thing.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving the four operations with rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve real and math problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division with rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Convert a rational number to a decimal using long division; know that the decimal form of a rational number terminates in 0s or eventually repeats.
description_plain: Your child can turn a fraction into a decimal by dividing and knows that the decimal either ends or repeats.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to multiply and divide rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to multiply and divide rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand that integers can be divided, provided that the divisor is not zero, and every quotient of integers (with non-zero divisor) is a rational number. If p and q are integers, then -(p/q) = (-p)/q = p/(-q). Interpret quotients of rational numbers by describing real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child understands that dividing whole numbers (except by zero) gives rational numbers and can explain real-life examples of dividing rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand that multiplication is extended from fractions to rational numbers by requiring that operations continue to satisfy the properties of operations, particularly the distributive property, leading to products such as (-1)(-1) = 1 and the rules for multiplying signed numbers. Interpret products of rational numbers by describing real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child understands how multiplication works with rational numbers, including negatives, and can explain real-world examples.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division and of fractions to multiply and divide rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide rational numbers by building on what they know about fractions.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add and subtract rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to add and subtract rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand subtraction of rational numbers as adding the additive inverse, p - q = p + (-q). Show that the distance between two rational numbers on the number line is the absolute value of their difference, and apply this principle in real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child understands that subtracting a number is like adding its opposite and can find the distance between two numbers on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand p + q as the number located a distance |q| from p, in the positive or negative direction depending on whether q is positive or negative. Show that a number and its opposite have a sum of 0 (are additive inverses). Interpret sums of rational numbers by describing real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child understands adding positive and negative numbers as moving left or right on a number line and knows that a number plus its opposite equals zero.

subject: Math
description: Describe situations in which opposite quantities combine to make 0.
description_plain: Your child can describe situations where opposite amounts cancel each other out to make zero.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of addition and subtraction to add and subtract rational numbers; represent addition and subtraction on a horizontal or vertical number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract rational numbers and show these operations on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of operations with fractions to add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide rational numbers using what they know about fractions.

subject: Math
description: Use proportional relationships to solve multistep ratio and percent problems.
description_plain: Your child can use proportional relationships to solve multi-step problems involving ratios and percents.

subject: Math
description: Explain what a point (x, y) on the graph of a proportional relationship means in terms of the situation, with special attention to the points (0, 0) and (1, r) where r is the unit rate.
description_plain: Your child can explain what points on a graph mean in a proportional relationship, especially the points (0,0) and (1, r) where r is the unit rate.

subject: Math
description: Represent proportional relationships by equations.
description_plain: Your child can write equations that show proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Identify the constant of proportionality (unit rate) in tables, graphs, equations, diagrams, and verbal descriptions of proportional relationships.
description_plain: Your child can find the constant rate (unit rate) in tables, graphs, equations, pictures, or words about proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Decide whether two quantities are in a proportional relationship, e.g., by testing for equivalent ratios in a table or graphing on a coordinate plane and observing whether the graph is a straight line through the origin.
description_plain: Your child can decide if two quantities have a proportional relationship by checking if their ratios are equal or if their graph is a straight line through the origin.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and represent proportional relationships between quantities.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and show proportional relationships between quantities.

subject: Math
description: Compute unit rates associated with ratios of fractions, including ratios of lengths, areas and other quantities measured in like or different units.
description_plain: Your child can find unit rates for ratios involving fractions, like speed or area, and write them as complex fractions or simpler numbers.

subject: Math
description: Analyze proportional relationships and use them to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use ratios to solve problems in everyday life and math.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving area, volume and surface area of two- and three-dimensional objects composed of triangles, quadrilaterals, polygons, cubes, and right prisms.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about area, volume, and surface area of 2D and 3D shapes like triangles, polygons, cubes, and prisms.

subject: Math
description: Use facts about supplementary, complementary, vertical, and adjacent angles in a multi-step problem to write and solve simple equations for an unknown angle in a figure.
