description_plain: Your child knows the differences between how people speak and how they write in standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child chooses words and phrases to make their writing or speaking more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child looks up words in dictionaries or other references to check and fix spelling when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (e.g., word families, position-based spellings, syllable patterns, ending rules, meaningful word parts) in writing words.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling patterns and rules, like word families and syllables, to write words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words (e.g., sitting, smiled, cries, happiness).
description_plain: Your child spells common words correctly and knows how to add endings like -ing or -ed to base words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use possessives.
description_plain: Form and use possessives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child uses commas and quotation marks correctly when writing conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in addresses.
description_plain: Use commas in addresses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the right words in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reciprocal pronouns correctly.
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns like 'each other' and 'one another' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write legibly in cursive or joined italics, allowing margins and correct spacing between letters in a word and words in a sentence.
description_plain: Your child writes neatly in cursive or joined letters with proper spacing and margins.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences.
description_plain: Your child writes simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child uses words that compare things, like 'bigger' or 'biggest,' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.
description_plain: Your child makes sure subjects and verbs agree and uses pronouns correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple (e.g., I walked; I walk; I will walk) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child uses past, present, and future verb tenses correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child uses both regular and irregular verbs correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns (e.g., childhood).
description_plain: Your child uses abstract nouns that name ideas or feelings, like 'childhood.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child uses both regular and irregular plural nouns correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in general and their functions in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs do in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and figures out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the text, breaking down word parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and chooses words and styles that fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child speaks in full sentences when needed to explain or give more details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create engaging audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child makes interesting audio recordings of stories or poems that are easy to understand and adds pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and deliver an informative/explanatory presentation on a topic that: organizes ideas around major points of information, follows a logical sequence, includes supporting details, uses clear and specific vocabulary, and provides a strong conclusion.
description_plain: Your child plans and gives a clear presentation on a topic with main points, details, good vocabulary, and a strong ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child tells about a topic, story, or experience using facts and details, speaking clearly and at a good speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what a speaker says, adding details when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas and important details when listening to stories or information in different formats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child explains their own ideas and what they understand during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand what others say, stay focused on the topic, and connect their ideas to what others have shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child will follow simple rules during discussions, like waiting their turn to speak, listening carefully, and talking one at a time about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready by reading or studying the material and uses what they learned to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can join different kinds of group talks, listen to others, build on their ideas, and clearly share their own thoughts about grade 3 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child adjusts how they speak for different situations and uses formal English when appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and the style fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child judges a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different media like visuals, numbers, and speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child prepares for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and convincingly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child remembers information from experiences or finds facts from books and websites, takes short notes, and organizes the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use technology to produce and publish writing (using keyboarding skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child uses computers or tablets to write and share their work and to work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child plans, revises, and edits their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: With adult support, your child writes pieces that are organized and fit the purpose of the task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Provide a sense of closure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'first,' 'then,' and 'finally' to show the order of events in their stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses conversations and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to show what happens and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by introducing characters and setting up events that happen in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events with clear details and a clear sequence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child adds a closing sentence or section to finish their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'also,' 'and,' and 'but' to connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, and details to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic and groups related information together, using pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain a topic clearly and share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing sentence or section to finish their opinion piece.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., because, therefore, since, for example) to connect opinion and reasons.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'because' and 'for example' to connect their opinion with reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure that lists reasons.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic, states their opinion, and organizes reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces that share their point of view and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over long and short times for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use details from stories or information texts to support their thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if they are trustworthy and accurate, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects by focusing on questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support claims about important topics using good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know and rereads when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings
description_plain: Your child reads stories and poems out loud with accuracy, good speed, and expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-appropriate words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multisyllable words.
description_plain: Decode multisyllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child reads words with common Latin endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child knows common beginnings and endings of words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words both in isolation and in text.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word parts to read words in and out of stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 2—3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child reads and understands grade 2–3 level nonfiction texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts the main points and details in two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text (e.g., comparison, cause/effect, first/second/third in a sequence).
description_plain: Your child explains how sentences and paragraphs in a text are connected, like showing cause and effect or order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations (e.g., maps, photographs) and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text (e.g., where, when, why, and how key events occur).
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and words in a text to understand where, when, why, and how important events happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the author of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own opinion and the author’s point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools (e.g., key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses text features like headings and search tools to quickly find information on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area. (See grade 3 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of important words related to grade 3 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child describes how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected using words about time and cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount the key details and explain how they support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, tell important details, and explain how those details support the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a text by looking back at the text for answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 2—3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems for third grade on their own and connect what they read to their own experiences or other texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters (e.g., in books from a series).
description_plain: Your child can compare and tell how themes, settings, and plots are alike or different in stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (e.g., create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures in a story help show feelings, characters, or settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.
description_plain: Your child understands how their own opinion is different from the narrator’s or characters’ points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene, and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child can talk or write about parts of stories, plays, or poems using words like chapter, scene, or stanza, and explain how each part adds to the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral language. (See grade 3 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when words are used in a literal or a special way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story (e.g., their traits, motivations, or feelings) and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters’ traits, feelings, and reasons for acting, and explain how their actions move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the central message, lesson, or moral and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories like fables and myths from different cultures and explain the lesson or message using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a story by looking back at the text for answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar ideas or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence in a text and decide if they make sense and are enough to support the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can use and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a text, like sentences or chapters, connect to each other and to the whole text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or technical meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make good guesses about what it means, using parts of the text to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal contrast, addition, and other logical relationships (e.g., however, although, nevertheless, similarly, moreover, in addition).
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words that show ideas like contrast and addition, such as however, although, and moreover.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonyms, antonyms, homographs) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain common sayings and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain similes and metaphors in the context of what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases and to identify alternate word choices in all content areas.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses, both in books and online, to find word meanings, pronunciations, and other word choices.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., photograph, photosynthesis).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek and Latin roots and prefixes to help figure out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., cause/effect relationships and comparisons in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the text, like cause and effect or comparisons, to understand the meaning of words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 5 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the varieties of English (e.g., dialects, registers) used in stories, dramas, or poems.
description_plain: Your child can compare different types of English, like dialects or formal and informal language, used in stories, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer, shorter, or more interesting to improve meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-appropriate words correctly and use references to help when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to show titles of books, stories, or other works.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to set off the words yes and no (e.g., Yes, thank you), to set off a tag question from the rest of the sentence (e.g., It's true, isn't it?), and to indicate direct address (e.g., Is that you, Steve?).
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly with words like yes and no, in tag questions, and when directly addressing someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma after an introductory word or phrase at the start of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation to separate items in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas to separate items in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correlative conjunctions (e.g., either/or, neither/nor).
description_plain: Your child can use paired conjunctions like either/or and neither/nor correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and fix mistakes when verb tenses change incorrectly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verb tense to convey various times, sequences, states, and conditions.
description_plain: Your child can use verb tenses to show different times, sequences, and conditions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the perfect (e.g., I had walked; I have walked; I will have walked) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can form and use perfect verb tenses like 'had walked' or 'have walked.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections do and how they work in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on skills learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and figures out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the text, breaking down word parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and chooses words and styles that fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation, using formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, sound) and visual displays in presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, sounds, or other media to presentations to help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Memorize and recite a poem or section of a speech or historical document using rate, expression, and gestures appropriate to the selection.
description_plain: Your child can memorize and recite a poem, speech, or historical text using proper speed, expression, and gestures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and deliver an opinion speech that: states an opinion, logically sequences evidence to support the speaker's position, uses transition words to effectively link opinions and evidence (e.g., consequently and therefore), and provides a concluding statement related to the speaker's position.
description_plain: Your child can plan and give a speech stating an opinion, supporting it with reasons and transition words, and ending with a clear conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text or present an opinion, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can report on a topic or share an opinion clearly, using facts and details in order, and speak at a pace that is easy to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the points a speaker or media source makes and explain how each claim is supported by reasons and evidence, and identify and analyze any logical fallacies.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main points a speaker or media source makes, show how each point is supported by reasons and evidence, and spot any mistakes in logic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information from a text read aloud or from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and draw conclusions in light of information and knowledge gained from the discussions.
description_plain: Your child understands the main ideas shared in a discussion and can make conclusions based on what was talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions by making comments that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions during a discussion and add to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child follows the agreed rules during discussions and does the roles they are assigned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying the topic and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 5 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of discussions with others, listens to their ideas, and shares their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child adjusts how they speak for different situations and uses formal English when appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and the style fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child judges a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different media like visuals, numbers, and speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child prepares for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and convincingly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 Reading standards to informational texts (e.g., "Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point[s]").
description_plain: Your child explains how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or a drama, drawing on specific details in the text [e.g., how characters interact]").
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts characters, settings, or events in stories or plays using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses information from stories or informational texts to support their thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; summarize or paraphrase information in notes and finished work, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child remembers or finds important information from experiences, books, or the internet, summarizes it, and lists where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that use several sources to build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects using several sources to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of two pages in a single sitting.
description_plain: With some help, your child uses technology like the internet to write, share their work, and work with others, while showing good keyboard skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With help from others, your child plans, revises, edits, or rewrites their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing (including multiple paragraph texts) in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clear and organized texts with multiple paragraphs that fit the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows from the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses specific words and sensory details to clearly describe experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words, phrases, and clauses to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, description, and pacing, to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue, description, and pacing to develop events and show how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters or narrators, organizing events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined experiences using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child writes a concluding statement or section that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within and across categories of information using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., in contrast, especially).
description_plain: Your child connects ideas within and between categories using words and phrases like 'in contrast' or 'especially.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, provide a general observation and focus, and group related information logically; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly, gives a general idea, organizes related information logically, and uses headings, pictures, or multimedia when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic and share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that relates to the opinion they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., consequently, specifically).
description_plain: Your child connects their opinion and reasons using words like 'consequently' or 'specifically.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide logically ordered reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons in a logical order that are supported by facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which ideas are logically grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic or text clearly, states their opinion, and organizes ideas logically to support their purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting their point of view with reasons and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over long and short times for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use details from stories or information texts to support their thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if they are trustworthy and accurate, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects by focusing on questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support claims about important topics using good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses the words around an unknown word to figure out or correct its meaning and pronunciation, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level stories and poems out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts with a clear purpose and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of letter sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new multisyllable words correctly in and out of context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level phonics and word skills to figure out words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 4—5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand challenging informational texts about history, science, and technical subjects on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from different books or articles about the same topic and use it to talk or write clearly about that topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point(s).
description_plain: Your child understands how an author uses reasons and facts to support their ideas and can point out which reasons go with which ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw on information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question quickly or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can find answers or solve problems quickly by using information from several books, websites, or other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child can look at different stories about the same event and notice important similarities and differences in how people see it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in two or more texts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts are organized, like by order of events, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 5 topic or subject area. (See grade 5 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to school subjects or topics they are learning about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two or more people, events, or ideas are connected based on details in a history, science, math, or technical text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how details support them, and tell the main points in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can quote parts of a text accurately when explaining what it says and when making guesses based on the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 4—5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems for their grade level on their own and connect what they read to their own experiences or other texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre (e.g., mysteries and adventure stories) on their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories that are the same type, like mysteries or adventures, and talk about how they handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone, or beauty of a text (e.g., graphic novel, multimedia presentation of fiction, folktale, myth, poem).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures, videos, or other media add to the meaning, feeling, or beauty of a story, poem, or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influences how events are described.
description_plain: Your child understands how the narrator’s or speaker’s point of view changes the way events are described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes, or stanzas fits together to provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child can explain how chapters, scenes, or parts of a poem fit together to make the whole story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language such as metaphors and similes. (See grade 5 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when they are used in a special way like similes, metaphors, or idioms.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., how characters interact).
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more characters, places, or events in a story by using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text, including how characters in a story or drama respond to challenges or how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem by looking at details and can tell the story in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can quote parts of a story or poem accurately when explaining what it says and when making guesses based on the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar ideas or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence in a text and decide if they make sense and are enough to support the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can use and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a text, like sentences or chapters, connect to each other and to the whole text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or technical meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make good guesses about what it means, using parts of the text to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being (e.g., quizzed, whined, stammered) and that are basic to a particular topic (e.g., wildlife, conservation, and endangered when discussing animal preservation).
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words that are important for school subjects and that show clear actions, feelings, or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their opposites (antonyms) and to words with similar but not identical meanings (synonyms).
description_plain: Your child understands words better by learning their opposites and words that mean similar things but are not exactly the same.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child knows the meanings of common sayings, like idioms, proverbs, and adages.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors (e.g., as pretty as a picture) in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain simple similes and metaphors, like 'as pretty as a picture,' by using clues from the sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases and to identify alternate word choices in all content areas.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses, both in books and online, to find how to say words, what they mean, and other words to use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., telegraph, photograph, autograph).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek and Latin word parts to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., definitions, examples, or restatements in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence, like definitions or examples, to understand the meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 4 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown words or words with more than one meaning by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion).
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal English, like when giving a presentation, and when informal talk is okay, like in small group chats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose punctuation for effect.
description_plain: Your child can choose punctuation marks to make their writing clearer or more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child picks words and phrases that clearly express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when they write, speak, read, or listen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child spells grade-level words correctly and uses resources like dictionaries when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma before a coordinating conjunction in a compound sentence.
description_plain: Your child uses a comma before words like 'and' or 'but' when joining two sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks to mark direct speech and quotations from a text.
description_plain: Your child uses commas and quotation marks correctly when writing down what someone says.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct capitalization.
description_plain: Use correct capitalization.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write fluidly and legibly in cursive or joined italics.
description_plain: Your child writes neatly and smoothly in cursive or joined handwriting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly use frequently confused words (e.g., to, too, two; there, their).
description_plain: Your child uses commonly confused words correctly, like 'to,' 'too,' and 'two.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate fragments and run-ons.
description_plain: Your child writes complete sentences and fixes sentence fragments or run-on sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use prepositional phrases.
description_plain: Your child can make and use prepositional phrases in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns (e.g., a small red bag rather than a red small bag).
description_plain: Your child puts adjectives in the right order in sentences, like saying 'a small red bag' instead of 'a red small bag.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use modal auxiliaries (e.g., can, may, must) to convey various conditions.
description_plain: Your child can use words like can, may, and must to show different possibilities or needs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the progressive (e.g., I was walking; I am walking; I will be walking) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use verb forms that show actions happening now, in the past, or in the future, like 'I am walking' or 'I was walking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use interrogative, relative pronouns (who, whose, whom, which, that) and relative adverbs (where, when, why).
description_plain: Your child can use question words like who, which, and where to ask questions or give more information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and figures out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the text, breaking down word parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and chooses words and styles that fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion); use formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal language, like during presentations, and when informal talk is okay, like in small groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add audio recordings and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, sounds, or videos to presentations to help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and deliver a narrative presentation that: relates ideas, observations, or recollections; provides a clear context; and includes clear insight into why the event or experience is memorable.
description_plain: Your child can plan and tell a story that explains what happened, where and when it happened, and why it was important.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can clearly tell about a topic, story, or experience using facts and details, speaking at a pace others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker or media source provides to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain reasons and evidence that a speaker or media uses to support their points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
