description_plain: Your child can restate information they hear or see in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can explain their own ideas and understanding after listening to a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information, and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to understand better and add helpful comments during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules during discussions and do their assigned roles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 4 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different kinds of group talks, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child adjusts how they speak for different situations and uses formal English when appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and the style fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child judges a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different media like visuals, numbers, and speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child prepares for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and convincingly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to informational texts (e.g., "Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text").
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text [e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions].").
description_plain: Your child can describe characters, settings, or events in stories using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use information from stories or facts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; take notes, paraphrase, and categorize information, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can remember or find information from books or the internet, take notes, organize it, and list where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of one page in a single sitting.
description_plain: With some help, your child can use computers and the internet to write, share work, and work with others while typing well enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from others, your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing (including multiple-paragraph texts) in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and organized pieces with multiple paragraphs that fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that fits the story or experience they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear words and details that help others picture their experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses words like first, next, and finally to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue and descriptions to show what happened and how characters felt.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situationand introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tells events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child can write a conclusion that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within categories of information using words and phrases (e.g., another, for example, also, because).
description_plain: Your child connects ideas using words like also, for example, and because.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, or quotes to explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly and organizes related information into sections with helpful headings or pictures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that matches the opinion they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases (e.g., for instance, in order to, in addition).
description_plain: Your child connects their opinion and reasons using words like for example and in addition.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons supported by facts and details to explain their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, state their opinion, and organize related ideas to support their purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information
description_plain: Your child will write opinion pieces that give reasons and information to support their point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over long and short times for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use details from stories or information texts to support their thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if they are trustworthy and accurate, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects by focusing on questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support claims about important topics using good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the text to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of letter sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new, longer words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level phonics and word skills to read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 4—5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade-level informational texts about history, science, and technical topics, with help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from two texts on the same topic to talk or write about it clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, time lines, animations, or interactive elements on Web pages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain information shown in charts, graphs, or pictures and how it helps explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can compare a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event and explain how they focus on different details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, like by time order, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area. (See grade 4 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to grade 4 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain events or ideas in history, science, or technical texts, including what happened and why, using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how key details support it, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make guesses about what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in the grades 4—5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade-level stories, dramas, and poems, making connections to their own experiences or other texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and topics (e.g., opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (e.g., the quest) in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare how similar themes and story patterns are shown in stories and myths from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text, identifying where each version reflects specific descriptions and directions in the text.
description_plain: Your child can connect a story or play to its visual or oral version, pointing out where they match.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child can compare different points of view in stories, like first-person versus third-person narration.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between poems, drama, and prose, and refer to the structural elements of poems (e.g., verse, rhythm, meter) and drama (e.g., casts of characters, settings, descriptions, dialogue, stage directions) when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between poems, plays, and stories, and talk about parts like rhythm in poems or dialogue in plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including those that allude to significant characters found in mythology (e.g., Herculean). (See grade 4 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including those that refer to famous mythological characters, and explain how figurative language makes the text better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, setting, or event in detail using specific parts from the story or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme of a story, play, or poem using details from the text and summarize it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar ideas or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence in a text and decide if they make sense and are enough to support the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can use and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a text, like sentences or chapters, connect to each other and to the whole text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or technical meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make good guesses about what it means, using parts of the text to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use grade-appropriate academic and subject-specific words and can research new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., refined, respectful, polite, diplomatic, condescending).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the feelings or ideas that similar words suggest.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., literary, biblical, and mythological allusions) in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand figures of speech, like references to literature or myths, by looking at the context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at the context or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech or trace the etymology of words.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses, both print and online, to find how to say a word, its meaning, part of speech, or history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., belligerent, bellicose, rebel).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin roots and prefixes to help figure out the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the meaning of the sentence or the word’s role in it to help understand new words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose language that expresses ideas precisely and concisely, recognizing and eliminating wordiness and redundancy.
description_plain: Your child can choose words that express ideas clearly and briefly, avoiding extra or repeated words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives (e.g., It was a fascinating, enjoyable movie but not He wore an old[,] green shirt).
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly to separate adjectives that equally describe a noun.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Place phrases and clauses within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child can put phrases and clauses in the right place in sentences and fix confusing or misplaced parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child can choose different types of sentences to show how ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what phrases and clauses do in sentences and can explain their roles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and figures out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the text, breaking down word parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and chooses words and styles that fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.
description_plain: Your child includes pictures, videos, or other media in presentations to make their points clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Plan and present an argument that: supports a claim, acknowledges counterarguments, organizes evidence logically, uses words and phrases to create cohesion, and provides a concluding statement that supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child plans and shares an argument that supports their claim, considers opposing views, organizes evidence clearly, uses linking words, and ends with a strong conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings (e.g., argument, narrative, summary presentations), emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child presents ideas clearly and in order, using facts and examples, while making good eye contact and speaking loudly and clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, and attitude toward the subject, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker's argument and opinions, and decide if their reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas and details in different types of media and explain how they help understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.
description_plain: Your child listens to new information from others and changes their own ideas when it makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant observations and ideas that bring the discussion back on topic as needed.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to get more details and responds to others with helpful comments that keep the discussion on track.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group talks, keeps track of goals and deadlines, and knows their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses evidence to explore and think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 7 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child adjusts how they speak for different situations and uses formal English when appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow the ideas and the style fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child judges a speaker’s point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different media like visuals, numbers, and speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child prepares for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and convincingly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long times for different school subjects, purposes, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence from informational texts to support their analysis, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online, check if sources are trustworthy, and use others' ideas correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking more focused questions to explore.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write and share their work clearly and effectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to better reach their audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized in a way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports and follows from the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style and keeps an objective tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific language and subject-related words to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words to connect ideas and make their writing flow smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child develops a topic using well-chosen facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly, organizes ideas into categories, and uses headings, charts, or multimedia to help understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain history events, science experiments, or technical steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from or supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the argument they presented.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child supports their claims with logical reasons and accurate evidence from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child introduces claims about a topic, recognizes opposing claims, and organizes reasons and evidence logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about topics in specific school subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g. "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims").
description_plain: Your child reads nonfiction and checks if the arguments and evidence make sense and support the claims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 7 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history").
description_plain: Your child compares stories and historical facts to understand how fiction changes real events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses information from stories or facts to support their thinking, writing, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and the internet, checks if sources are trustworthy, and uses that information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects using several sources and asks new questions to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and link to and cite sources as well as to interact and collaborate with others, including linking to and citing sources.
description_plain: Your child uses technology to write, share their work, and connect with others, including citing sources properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to better reach their audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that reflects on the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to capture the action and convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and details that help readers picture the action and feel the experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue and description to develop events and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, and organizes events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports and follows the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transitions to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, and examples to explain their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or thesis statement clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly, organizes ideas using methods like comparison or cause and effect, and uses headings or visuals to help understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes using a formal style when appropriate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use connecting words and phrases to make their writing clear and show how their reasons and evidence relate to their claim.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) or counterarguments with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child will support their opinions or opposing views with clear reasons and trustworthy evidence, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge and address alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main opinion, recognize other viewpoints, and organize their reasons and evidence in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that clearly explain their opinions with good reasons and supporting facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over long and short times for different tasks, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revising.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find and use details from stories or information texts to support their thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from many sources, check if they are trustworthy and accurate, and use the information without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do both short and longer research projects by focusing on questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing important content.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support claims about important topics using good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of 8th grade, your child can read and understand science and technical texts for grades 6 to 8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare information from experiments, videos, or simulations with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions based on research, and guesses in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child can combine information given in words with pictures, charts, or graphs to understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can understand why an author explains something, describes a process, or talks about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes a text and how its parts help explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6—8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what symbols and special words mean when reading science or technical texts for grades 6–8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child can carefully follow multi-step instructions when doing experiments, measuring, or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or conclusions in a text and give a clear summary without mixing in their own opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can point to exact parts of a science or technical text to support their understanding and analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of 8th grade, your child can read and understand history and social studies texts for grades 6 to 8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare and explain how a primary source and a secondary source on the same topic relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and thoughtful judgments in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can use information from pictures, charts, graphs, videos, or maps together with written or digital texts to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child can spot parts of a text that show the author's point of view or purpose, like strong language or what facts are included or left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally).
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text shares information, such as in order, by comparing, or by showing cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in history or social studies texts, including special vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child can identify the important steps in a history or social studies process described in a text, like how a bill becomes a law.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or important information in a primary or secondary source and give a clear summary without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child can point to exact parts of primary and secondary sources to support their analysis.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles for grades 6–8, getting help when the text is harder.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more authors write about the same topic by looking at the different facts or ideas they focus on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the arguments and claims in a text to see if the reasons and evidence really support them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text to an audio, video, or multimedia version of the text, analyzing each medium's portrayal of the subject (e.g., how the delivery of a speech affects the impact of the words).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written text to its audio, video, or multimedia version and explain how each way of sharing the story affects its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her position from that of others.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view or purpose and explain how the author shows their opinion is different from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the use of text features (e.g., graphics, headers, captions) in public documents.
description_plain: Your child can look at parts of public documents like pictures, headings, and captions to understand how they help explain the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes a text and how the main sections help develop the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone. (See grade 7 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in a text (e.g., how ideas influence individuals or events, or how individuals influence ideas or events).
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas in a text influence each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas in a text and analyze their development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how they develop, and give a clear summary without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find several examples from the text to support what it says directly and what can be understood from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand nonfiction stories and articles for grades 6–8, with help when the text is harder.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child can compare a made-up story about a time or place with a real historical account to see how fiction changes history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version, analyzing the effects of techniques unique to each medium (e.g., lighting, sound, color, or camera focus and angles in a film).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written story, play, or poem to its audio, video, or live version and explain the differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the points of view of different characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters' or narrators' points of view and how they contrast.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a drama's or poem's form or structure (e.g., soliloquy, sonnet) contributes to its meaning.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the form or structure of a play or poem helps show its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of rhymes and other repetitions of sounds (e.g., alliteration) on a specific verse or stanza of a poem or section of a story or drama. (See grade 7 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including special meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular elements of a story or drama interact (e.g., how setting shapes the characters or plot).
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story or play, like setting and characters, work together.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea of a story and explain how it develops, then give a clear summary without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find several examples from the story or play to support what it says directly and what can be understood from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar ideas or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence in a text and decide if they make sense and are enough to support the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can use and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a text, like sentences or chapters, connect to each other and to the whole text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or technical meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make good guesses about what it means, using parts of the text to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use academic and subject-specific words correctly for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at a college level, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use academic and subject-specific words correctly for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at a college level, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain small differences in meaning between similar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain small differences in meaning between similar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., hyperbole, paradox) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like exaggerations or contradictions and how they add meaning to a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., hyperbole, paradox) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech like exaggerations or contradictions and how they add meaning to a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check if their first guess about a word's meaning is correct by looking at the context or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check if their first guess about a word's meaning is correct by looking at the context or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., college-level dictionaries, rhyming dictionaries, bilingual dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, its etymology, or its standard usage.
description_plain: Your child can use different reference books or online tools to find how to say a word, what it means, its part of speech, origin, or correct use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., college-level dictionaries, rhyming dictionaries, bilingual dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, its etymology, or its standard usage.
description_plain: Your child can use different reference books or online tools to find how to say a word, what it means, its part of speech, origin, or correct use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., conceive, conception, conceivable). Apply knowledge of Greek, Latin, and Anglo-Saxon roots and affixes to draw inferences concerning the meaning of scientific and mathematical terminology.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use word parts and patterns to understand different meanings or parts of speech, including scientific and math terms.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., conceive, conception, conceivable). Apply knowledge of Greek, Latin, and Anglo-Saxon roots and affixes to draw inferences concerning the meaning of scientific and mathematical terminology.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use word parts and patterns to understand different meanings or parts of speech, including scientific and math terms.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the surrounding words and sentence meaning to figure out the meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence, paragraph, or text; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the surrounding words and sentence meaning to figure out the meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 11—12 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grades 11—12 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary syntax for effect, consulting references (e.g., Tufte's Artful Sentences) for guidance as needed; apply an understanding of syntax to the study of complex texts when reading.
description_plain: Your child can change sentence structure for effect and use resources to help, applying this understanding when reading complex texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary syntax for effect, consulting references (e.g., Tufte's Artful Sentences) for guidance as needed; apply an understanding of syntax to the study of complex texts when reading.
