description_plain: Your child can change sentence structure for effect and use resources to help, applying this understanding when reading complex texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly when reading, writing, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly when reading, writing, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Observe hyphenation conventions.
description_plain: Your child knows and follows the rules for using hyphens correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Observe hyphenation conventions.
description_plain: Your child knows and follows the rules for using hyphens correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Resolve issues of complex or contested usage, consulting references (e.g., Merriam-Webster's Dictionary of English Usage, Garner's Modern American Usage) as needed.
description_plain: Your child can solve tricky language usage problems by checking trusted references when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Resolve issues of complex or contested usage, consulting references (e.g., Merriam-Webster's Dictionary of English Usage, Garner's Modern American Usage) as needed.
description_plain: Your child can solve tricky language usage problems by checking trusted references when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply the understanding that usage is a matter of convention, can change over time, and is sometimes contested.
description_plain: Your child understands that language rules can change over time and sometimes people disagree about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply the understanding that usage is a matter of convention, can change over time, and is sometimes contested.
description_plain: Your child understands that language rules can change over time and sometimes people disagree about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and build on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and build on what they learned before.

description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of general and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and figures out new words on their own.

description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the text, breaking down word parts, or looking them up.

description: Determine two or more central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to provide a complex analysis; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child finds two or more main ideas in a text, explains how they grow and connect, and summarizes the text fairly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.
description_plain: Your child uses strong evidence from the text to explain what it says clearly and to support ideas they infer, including noticing unclear parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.
description_plain: Your child uses strong evidence from the text to explain what it says clearly and to support ideas they infer, including noticing unclear parts.

subject: Language Arts
description:
By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands challenging literature like stories, plays, and poems by grade 12, making personal and ethical connections to deepen understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description:
By the end of grade 11, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 11—CCR text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
By the end of grade 12, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, at the high end of the grades 11—CCR text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands challenging literature like stories, plays, and poems by grade 12, making personal and ethical connections to deepen understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of eighteenth-, nineteenth- and early-twentieth-century foundational works of American literature, including how two or more texts from the same period treat similar themes or topics.
description_plain: Your child knows important American literature from the 1700s to early 1900s and compares how different texts from that time handle similar themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of eighteenth-, nineteenth- and early-twentieth-century foundational works of American literature, including how two or more texts from the same period treat similar themes or topics.
description_plain: Your child knows important American literature from the 1700s to early 1900s and compares how different texts from that time handle similar themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple interpretations of a story, drama, or poem (e.g., recorded or live production of a play or recorded novel or poetry), evaluating how each version interprets the source text. (Include at least one play by Shakespeare and one play by an American dramatist.)
description_plain: Your child compares different versions of a story, play, or poem, including works by Shakespeare and American playwrights, to see how each interprets the original.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple interpretations of a story, drama, or poem (e.g., recorded or live production of a play or recorded novel or poetry), evaluating how each version interprets the source text. (Include at least one play by Shakespeare and one play by an American dramatist.)
description_plain: Your child compares different versions of a story, play, or poem, including works by Shakespeare and American playwrights, to see how each interprets the original.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a case in which grasping point of view requires distinguishing what is directly stated in a text from what is really meant (e.g., satire, sarcasm, irony, or understatement).
description_plain: Your child understands when a text means more than what it says directly, like in satire or irony.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a case in which grasping point of view requires distinguishing what is directly stated in a text from what is really meant (e.g., satire, sarcasm, irony, or understatement).
description_plain: Your child understands when a text means more than what it says directly, like in satire or irony.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text (e.g., the choice of where to begin or end a story, the choice to provide a comedic or tragic resolution) contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how an author's choices about story parts, like where it starts or ends, affect the whole story's meaning and feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text (e.g., the choice of where to begin or end a story, the choice to provide a comedic or tragic resolution) contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.
description_plain: Your child analyzes how an author's choices about story parts, like where it starts or ends, affect the whole story's meaning and feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including words with multiple meanings or language that is particularly fresh, engaging, or beautiful. (Include Shakespeare as well as other authors.) (See grade 11–12 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of words and phrases in literature, including figurative language, and explains how word choices affect tone and meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including words with multiple meanings or language that is particularly fresh, engaging, or beautiful. (Include Shakespeare as well as other authors.) (See grade 11–12 Language standards 4–6 for additional expectations.)
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of words and phrases in literature, including figurative language, and explains how word choices affect tone and meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of the author's choices regarding how to develop and relate elements of a story or drama (e.g., where a story is set, how the action is ordered, how the characters/archetypes are introduced and developed).
description_plain: Your child analyzes how an author develops story elements like setting, plot order, and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of the author's choices regarding how to develop and relate elements of a story or drama (e.g., where a story is set, how the action is ordered, how the characters/archetypes are introduced and developed).
description_plain: Your child analyzes how an author develops story elements like setting, plot order, and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child finds two or more themes in a text, explains how they develop and connect, and summarizes the text objectively.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account; provide an objective summary of the text.
description_plain: Your child finds two or more themes in a text, explains how they develop and connect, and summarizes the text objectively.

description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.
description_plain: Your child uses strong evidence from the text to explain what it says and to support ideas they infer, including noticing unclear parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain.
description_plain: Your child uses strong evidence from the text to explain what it says and to support ideas they infer, including noticing unclear parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar ideas or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar ideas or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence in a text and decide if they make sense and are enough to support the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence in a text and decide if they make sense and are enough to support the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can use and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can use and think about information from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author’s point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a text, like sentences or chapters, connect to each other and to the whole text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a text, like sentences or chapters, connect to each other and to the whole text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or technical meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or technical meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make good guesses about what it means, using parts of the text to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and make good guesses about what it means, using parts of the text to explain their ideas.

subject: Math
description: Describe qualitatively the functional relationship between two quantities by analyzing a graph (e.g., where the function is increasing or decreasing, linear or nonlinear). Sketch a graph that exhibits the qualitative features of a function that has been described verbally.
description_plain: Your child can describe how two quantities relate by looking at a graph and can draw a graph based on a verbal description of a function.

subject: Math
description: Construct a function to model a linear relationship between two quantities. Determine the rate of change and initial value of the function from a description of a relationship or from two (x, y) values, including reading these from a table or from a graph. Interpret the rate of change and initial value of a linear function in terms of the situation it models, and in terms of its graph or a table of values.
description_plain: Your child can create a function to show a straight-line relationship, find the rate of change and starting value from descriptions, tables, or graphs, and explain what they mean.

subject: Math
description: Use functions to model relationships between quantities.
description_plain: Your child uses functions to show how different amounts are connected.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the equation y = mx + b as defining a linear function, whose graph is a straight line; give examples of functions that are not linear.
description_plain: Your child understands that y = mx + b is a rule for a straight-line function and can give examples of functions that are not straight lines.

subject: Math
description: Compare properties of two functions each represented in a different way (algebraically, graphically, numerically in tables, or by verbal descriptions).
description_plain: Your child compares two functions shown in different ways, like tables or equations, to decide which changes faster.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a function is a rule that assigns to each input exactly one output. The graph of a function is the set of ordered pairs consisting of an input and the corresponding output.
description_plain: Your child understands that a function assigns one output to each input and can recognize this on a graph.

subject: Math
description: Define, evaluate, and compare functions.
description_plain: Your child can define what functions are, find their values, and compare different functions.

subject: Math
description: Understand that patterns of association can also be seen in bivariate categorical data by displaying frequencies and relative frequencies in a two-way table. Construct and interpret a two-way table summarizing data on two categorical variables collected from the same subjects. Use relative frequencies calculated for rows or columns to describe possible association between the two variables.
description_plain: Your child can make and read two-way tables that show how two categories relate and use these tables to describe possible connections.

subject: Math
description: Use the equation of a linear model to solve problems in the context of bivariate measurement data, interpreting the slope and intercept.
description_plain: Your child can use a linear equation to solve problems with two related measurements and understand what the slope and intercept mean.

subject: Math
description: Know that straight lines are widely used to model relationships between two quantitative variables. For scatter plots that suggest a linear association, informally fit a straight line, and informally assess the model fit by judging the closeness of the data points to the line.
description_plain: Your child knows that straight lines can show relationships between two numbers and can draw a line that fits data points on a scatter plot.

subject: Math
description: Construct and interpret scatter plots for bivariate measurement data to investigate patterns of association between two quantities. Describe patterns such as clustering, outliers, positive or negative association, linear association, and nonlinear association.
description_plain: Your child can make and read scatter plots to find patterns like groups, outliers, or whether two things increase or decrease together.

subject: Math
description: Investigate patterns of association in bivariate data.
description_plain: Your child can study how two sets of data relate to each other.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems leading to two linear equations in two variables.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems that involve two linear equations with two variables.

subject: Math
description: Solve systems of two linear equations in two variables algebraically, and estimate solutions by graphing the equations. Solve simple cases by inspection.
description_plain: Your child can solve two linear equations with two variables using algebra and estimate solutions by graphing, including recognizing when no solution exists.

subject: Math
description: Understand that solutions to a system of two linear equations in two variables correspond to points of intersection of their graphs, because points of intersection satisfy both equations simultaneously.
description_plain: Your child understands that solutions to two linear equations are where their graphs cross because those points satisfy both equations.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and solve pairs of simultaneous linear equations.
description_plain: Your child can solve two linear equations at the same time to find where they both work.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations with rational number coefficients, including equations whose solutions require expanding expressions using the distributive property and collecting like terms.
description_plain: Your child can solve linear equations that have fractions or decimals by expanding and combining terms.

subject: Math
description: Give examples of linear equations in one variable with one solution, infinitely many solutions, or no solutions. Show which of these possibilities is the case by successively transforming the given equation into simpler forms, until an equivalent equation of the form x = a, a = a, or a = b results (where a and b are different numbers).
description_plain: Your child can show examples of linear equations that have one solution, many solutions, or no solution by simplifying the equations step-by-step.

subject: Math
description: Solve linear equations in one variable.
description_plain: Your child can solve linear equations with one variable.

subject: Math
description: Analyze and solve linear equations and pairs of simultaneous linear equations.
description_plain: Your child understands how to solve single and pairs of linear equations.

subject: Math
description: Use similar triangles to explain why the slope m is the same between any two distinct points on a non-vertical line in the coordinate plane; derive the equation y = mx for a line through the origin and the equation y = mx + b for a line intercepting the vertical axis at b.
description_plain: Your child can explain why the slope is the same between any two points on a line and can write equations for lines through the origin or with a vertical intercept.

subject: Math
description: Graph proportional relationships, interpreting the unit rate as the slope of the graph. Compare two different proportional relationships represented in different ways.
description_plain: Your child can draw graphs of proportional relationships, understand the slope as the unit rate, and compare different proportional relationships.

subject: Math
description: Understand the connections between proportional relationships, lines, and linear equations.
description_plain: Your child understands how proportional relationships, lines, and straight-line equations are connected.

subject: Math
description: Perform operations with numbers expressed in scientific notation, including problems where both decimal and scientific notation are used. Use scientific notation and choose units of appropriate size for measurements of very large or very small quantities (e.g., use millimeters per year for seafloor spreading). Interpret scientific notation that has been generated by technology.
description_plain: Your child can work with very large or small numbers using scientific notation and choose the right units for measurements.

subject: Math
description: Use numbers expressed in the form of a single digit times an integer power of 10 to estimate very large or very small quantities, and to express how many times as much one is than the other.
description_plain: Your child can use numbers written as a digit times a power of 10 to estimate very big or small amounts and compare them.

subject: Math
description: Use square root and cube root symbols to represent solutions to equations of the form x² = p and x³ = p, where p is a positive rational number. Evaluate square roots of small perfect squares and cube roots of small perfect cubes. Know that √2 is irrational.
description_plain: Your child can use square roots and cube roots to solve equations and knows that some numbers like √2 cannot be written as simple fractions.

subject: Math
description: Know and apply the properties of integer exponents to generate equivalent numerical expressions.
description_plain: Your child knows the rules for exponents with whole numbers and can use them to rewrite expressions.

subject: Math
description: Work with radicals and integer exponents.
description_plain: Your child works with roots (like square roots) and whole number exponents.

subject: Math
description: Use rational approximations of irrational numbers to compare the size of irrational numbers, locate them approximately on a number line diagram, and estimate the value of expressions (e.g., π²).
description_plain: Your child can estimate and compare irrational numbers by using close rational numbers and place them roughly on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Know that numbers that are not rational are called irrational. Understand informally that every number has a decimal expansion; for rational numbers show that the decimal expansion repeats eventually, and convert a decimal expansion which repeats eventually into a rational number.
description_plain: Your child understands that irrational numbers are numbers that can't be written as fractions and can explain how decimals repeat for rational numbers.

subject: Math
description: Know that there are numbers that are not rational, and approximate them by rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child knows there are numbers that can't be written as fractions and can estimate them using fractions or decimals.

subject: Math
description: Know the formulas for the volumes of cones, cylinders, and spheres and use them to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child knows formulas to find the volume of cones, cylinders, and spheres and can use them to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving volume of cylinders, cones, and spheres.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems involving the volume of cylinders, cones, and spheres.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between two points in a coordinate system.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between two points on a coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to determine unknown side lengths in right triangles in real-world and mathematical problems in two and three dimensions.
description_plain: Your child can use the Pythagorean Theorem to find missing side lengths in right triangles in both 2D and 3D problems.

subject: Math
description: Explain a proof of the Pythagorean Theorem and its converse.
description_plain: Your child can explain why the Pythagorean Theorem works and also explain its reverse.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply the Pythagorean Theorem.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use the Pythagorean Theorem to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Use informal arguments to establish facts about the angle sum and exterior angle of triangles, about the angles created when parallel lines are cut by a transversal, and the angle-angle criterion for similarity of triangles.
description_plain: Your child can explain facts about triangle angles, angles made by parallel lines, and how to tell if triangles are similar.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a two-dimensional figure is similar to another if the second can be obtained from the first by a sequence of rotations, reflections, translations, and dilations; given two similar two-dimensional figures, describe a sequence that exhibits the similarity between them.
description_plain: Your child understands that two shapes are similar if one can be changed into the other by moving, flipping, or resizing, and can describe how to do this.

subject: Math
description: Describe the effect of dilations, translations, rotations, and reflections on two-dimensional figures using coordinates.
description_plain: Your child can describe how moving, flipping, turning, and resizing shapes changes their coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Understand that a two-dimensional figure is congruent to another if the second can be obtained from the first by a sequence of rotations, reflections, and translations; given two congruent figures, describe a sequence that exhibits the congruence between them.
description_plain: Your child understands that two shapes are congruent if one can be moved or flipped to match the other exactly, and can explain how.

subject: Math
description: Parallel lines are taken to parallel lines.
description_plain: Your child understands that parallel lines stay parallel after transformations.

subject: Math
description: Angles are taken to angles of the same measure.
description_plain: Your child understands that angles keep their size after transformations.

subject: Math
description: Lines are taken to lines, and line segments to line segments of the same length.
description_plain: Your child understands that lines and line segments keep their length after transformations.

subject: Math
description: Verify experimentally the properties of rotations, reflections, and translations:
description_plain: Your child can test and understand how shapes change when they are rotated, flipped, or slid.

subject: Math
description: Understand congruence and similarity using physical models, transparencies, or geometry software.
description_plain: Your child uses models or tools to understand when shapes are the same size and shape or just similar.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Science
description: Plan and carry out fair tests in which variables are controlled and failure points are considered to identify aspects of a model or prototype that can be improved.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do fair tests by controlling variables and thinking about what might go wrong to find ways to improve a model or prototype.

subject: Science
description: Generate and compare multiple possible solutions to a problem based on how well each is likely to meet the criteria and constraints of the problem.
description_plain: Your child can come up with and compare several possible solutions to a problem to see which ones best meet the goals and limits.

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem reflecting a need or a want that includes specified criteria for success and constraints on materials, time, or cost.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple design problem based on a need or want, including what success looks like and limits like materials, time, or cost.

subject: Science
description: Engineering Design
description_plain: Engineering Design

subject: Science
description: Define a simple design problem that can be solved by applying scientific ideas about magnets.
description_plain: Your child can describe a simple problem that can be solved using ideas about magnets.

subject: Science
description: Ask questions to determine cause and effect relationships of electric or magnetic interactions between two objects not in contact with each other.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to find out how electric or magnetic forces affect objects without touching.

subject: Science
description: Make observations and/or measurements of an object's motion to provide evidence that a pattern can be used to predict future motion.
description_plain: Your child can watch and measure how things move to find patterns that help predict future motion.

subject: Science
description: Plan and conduct an investigation to provide evidence of the effects of balanced and unbalanced forces on the motion of an object.
description_plain: Your child can plan and do an experiment to show how balanced and unbalanced forces change how things move.

subject: Science
description: Motion and Stability: Forces and Interactions
description_plain: Your child learns how things move and stay still because of forces pushing or pulling.

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a design solution that reduces the impacts of a weather-related hazard.
description_plain: Your child can explain why a design helps reduce problems caused by bad weather.

subject: Science
description: Earth and Human Activity
description_plain: Earth and Human Activity

subject: Science
description: Obtain and combine information to describe climates in different regions of the world.
description_plain: Your child will gather and combine information to describe different climates around the world.

subject: Science
description: Represent data in tables and graphical displays to describe typical weather conditions expected during a particular season.
description_plain: Your child can show weather data in tables and graphs to explain what weather is usually like in a season.

subject: Science
description: Earth's Systems
description_plain: Earth's Systems

subject: Science
description: Make a claim about the merit of a solution to a problem caused when the environment changes and the types of plants and animals that live there may change.
description_plain: Your child can explain if a solution is good for helping plants and animals when their environment changes.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument with evidence that in a particular habitat some organisms can survive well, some survive less well, and some cannot survive at all.
description_plain: Your child can explain with evidence why some animals live well in a habitat, some live less well, and some cannot live there at all.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to construct an explanation for how the variations in characteristics among individuals of the same species may provide advantages in surviving, finding mates, and reproducing.
description_plain: Your child understands how differences in animals or plants help them survive, find mates, and have babies.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data from fossils to provide evidence of the organisms and the environments in which they lived long ago.
description_plain: Your child can study fossils to learn about animals and environments from long ago.

subject: Science
description: Biological Evolution: Unity and Diversity
description_plain: Your child understands how living things have changed over time and how different kinds of plants and animals are related.

subject: Science
description: Use evidence to support the explanation that traits can be influenced by the environment.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence to show how the environment can change traits in living things.

subject: Science
description: Analyze and interpret data to provide evidence that plants and animals have traits inherited from parents and that variation of these traits exists in a group of similar organisms.
description_plain: Your child can look at data to show that plants and animals get traits from their parents and that these traits can vary.

subject: Science
description: Heredity: Inheritance and Variation of Traits
description_plain: Your child learns how traits are passed from parents to children and how they can vary.

subject: Science
description: Construct an argument that some animals form groups that help members survive.
description_plain: Your child can explain why some animals live in groups to help each other survive.

subject: Science
description: Ecosystems: Interactions, Energy, and Dynamics
description_plain: Your child explores how plants, animals, and their environment interact and depend on each other.

subject: Science
description: Develop models to describe that organisms have unique and diverse life cycles but all have in common birth, growth, reproduction, and death.
description_plain: Your child can create models to show that all living things have life stages like birth, growth, reproduction, and death.

subject: Science
description: From Molecules to Organisms: Structures and Processes
description_plain: Your child learns about the parts of plants and animals and how they help them live and grow.

subject: Science
description: Use models to describe that energy in animals' food (used for body repair, growth, motion, and to maintain body warmth) was once energy from the sun.
description_plain: Your child can use models to show that the energy animals get from food originally comes from the sun.

subject: Science
description: Energy
description_plain: Energy

subject: Science
description: Support an argument that the gravitational force exerted by Earth on objects is directed down.
description_plain: Your child can explain that Earth's gravity pulls objects straight down.

subject: Science
description: Motion and Stability: Forces and Interactions
